nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Criol
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alberto, Hipolito, Luis, Eriberto, Jose, Luisa, Ramon, Ricardo.  French/Haitian Edwige, Fritz, Serge, Yvrose.
Catalan: from the old personal name Oriol, from Latin Aureolus.

Catalan and French: nickname, possibly for a flamboyant dresser or sweet singer, from Catalan and Old French oriol ‘oriole’ (from Latin aureolus ‘golden’), a species of bird of which the male has bright yellow and black plumage. As a surname of French origin it is also common in Haiti.

variants.name: Oriel
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English and Old French female personal name Oriold, Oriel, from ancient Germanic Aurildis, Orieldis, composed of elements meaning ‘fire’ + ‘strife’.
French (Vosges): nickname from Old French oriel, a variant of oriol ‘oriole’ (see Oriol ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Criol was born on 29 September 1305, in Maidstone, Kent, England, his father, Sir Nicholas de Criol III, was 22 and his mother, Rosia De Criol, was 18. He had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Lettice. He died on 5 January 1376, in Dartford, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John de Criol est né le 29 septembre 1305, à Maidstone, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Sir Nicholas de Criol III, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Rosia De Criol, avait 18 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Lettice. Il est décédé le 5 janvier 1376, à Dartford, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John de Criol am 29. September 1305 in Maidstone, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Nicholas de Criol III 22 und seine Mutter Rosia De Criol 18. Er hatte mit Lettice mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 5. Januar 1376 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Dartford, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Criol è nato il il 29 settembre 1305 nel Maidstone, Kent, England, il suo padre, Sir Nicholas de Criol III, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Rosia De Criol, aveva 18 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Lettice. Morì il 5 gennaio 1376, nel Dartford, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 70 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Criol(이)가 1305년 9월 29일 영국 켄트 메이드스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Nicholas de Criol III(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Rosia De Criol(은)는 18세였다. 그는 Lettice(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1376년 1월 5일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 더르트퍼드에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Criol nasceu em 29 de setembro de 1305, em Maidstone, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Nicholas de Criol III, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Rosia De Criol, tinha 18 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Lettice. Ele faleceu em 5 de janeiro de 1376, em Dartford, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John de Criol родился 29 сентября 1305 г. в местности меидстоун, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Nicholas de Criol III было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Rosia De Criol, 18. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Lettice. Он умер 5 января 1376 г. в местности дартфорд, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет.

Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas con Lettice. Murió el 5 de enero de 1376, en Dartford, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 70 años.
